summary: Do not ship the plan yet. The committed RED evidence correctly captures the exact pass-abort symptom, but the revised witness gate still has two material defects. Simpler alternative: direct file-based discovery plus table-driven predicate tests. Open question: none.

findings.title: `pipefail` can turn a successful witness match into `MISSING WITNESS`
findings.body: The discovery check pipes `list_for` into `grep -qE` while `pipefail` is enabled. After an early match, `grep -q` exits and the upstream `cat` can receive SIGPIPE; the pipeline then returns 141 and takes the false `MISSING WITNESS` branch. This was reproduced with a matching first line followed by sufficient output. As the test list grows, a present mandatory witness can therefore block acceptance nondeterministically.
findings.recommendation: Cache each target’s list, check the cargo listing exit status, and run `grep -qE` directly against the cached file rather than through a pipeline. Add an early-match/large-list selftest.

findings.title: Two advertised verdict predicates are not actually regression-tested
findings.body: Cases `(d)` and `(f)` both pass cargo exit 101. Consequently, deleting or breaking the numeric `failed` check and the zero-result-line check still leaves all six selftests green because the independent nonzero-exit check supplies the expected failure. Thus `M-FAILED-10` and the no-results guard are not pinned as claimed; a masked or miswired cargo exit could make the live gate accept failed or absent suite output.
findings.recommendation: Run the 10-failed and zero-result fixtures with synthetic cargo exit 0, or directly assert their parsed tallies. Keep case `(e)` as the separate nonzero-exit witness.

next_steps: Repair discovery so successful matches cannot inherit upstream SIGPIPE status.
Make every `verdict` predicate independently load-bearing in `--selftest`.
